title: Eureka Seven copying Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Eureka Seven copying Evangelion?             Quote Post by Strayn » Fri Mar 11, 2011 12:45 pm I have noticed that Eureka Seven has quite a few similarities with Evangelion. Has anyone else noticed this, as well?

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Fri Mar 11, 2011 2:29 pm It's been quite common with mecha anime to sort of borrow or spoof Evangelion for a while. Otaku USA even dubbed it the "EVA Template" (referring to the act, not the show). However, depending on the team involved, it will be a moderate success or a flop.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Fri Mar 11, 2011 4:35 pm Well, Eureka Seven's chief writer (Dai Sato) has dismissed similarities between it and Evangelion, but the director (Tomoki Kyoda) has claimed that Evangelion is an influence for him. On my part, I think there's some definite lines of influence, particularly in regards to Eureka and Anemone, and there's a couple of scenes that seem like direct references. It's hardly something that can be called a rip-off though. Eureka Seven has its own distinguished mythology and general feel.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Mar 11, 2011 4:44 pm A comparison video, for those curious: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QDVaVRQoQhQ I've only seen a little bit of Eureka Seven , but from what I understand the "ripoffs" in question are more homage than plagiarism.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Mar 11, 2011 5:15 pm Isn't it more uncommon to find post- Evangelion mecha series that don't copy much from it?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Mar 11, 2011 7:20 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sat Mar 12, 2011 4:27 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: Even worse, the 'ripoffs' in display in that video are almost all from the exact same scene of Eureka Seven; one that IMO was very clearly homage due to the very heavy handed obviousness the poster decided to chop up into little bits giving a false impression of the show. A very disappointing video and argument.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Mar 12, 2011 5:13 am It's called influence, not "rip-off" Eureka 7 is overrun with similar legacy from other mecha shows like Gundam and Macross

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Mar 12, 2011 6:00 am Any mecha show that is "little boy suddenly gets thrown into world with mechs and stuff ANGST ANGST ANGST" resembles each other in one way or another. I always thought that E7 resembled Gundam more than Eva or any other mecha show.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Mar 12, 2011 11:31 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: I always thought that E7 resembled Gundam more than Eva or any other mecha show.

[]:             Quote Post by Strayn » Mon Mar 14, 2011 12:25 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: Any mecha show that is "little boy suddenly gets thrown into world with mechs and stuff ANGST ANGST ANGST" resembles each other in one way or another. I always thought that E7 resembled Gundam more than Eva or any other mecha show.

[]:             Quote Post by Guilt & Innocence » Mon Mar 14, 2011 12:50 am Eureka 7 being the only series that I give more affection to than Evangelion(Hides behind AT FIELD) I will defend it to the end. And most of those "clips" were from the same scene which is a homage to a berserk UNIT 01

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Mon Mar 14, 2011 5:59 am Guilt & Innocence wrote: Eureka 7 being the only series that I give more affection to than Evangelion(Hides behind AT FIELD) I will defend it to the end. And most of those "clips" were from the same scene which is a homage to a berserk UNIT 01

[]:             Quote Post by TaintedHero » Tue Mar 15, 2011 1:42 pm BornIn1142 wrote: Actually, what I really noticed about that scene was that military KLF being speared through the stomach and being held off the ground like Eva-02 in EoE. Not that it couldn't be a coincidence...

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Tue Mar 15, 2011 1:49 pm TaintedHero wrote: Hm... I can't recall that scene at all, it must not have stood out to me as much.

[]:             Quote Post by TaintedHero » Tue Mar 15, 2011 2:00 pm BornIn1142 wrote: It's in the video posted earlier, at about 0:29.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Mar 15, 2011 2:51 pm Strayn wrote: I didn't know Gundam had lifeforms that were discovered and created into mecha.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Tue Mar 15, 2011 4:48 pm Strayn wrote: I didn't know Gundam had lifeforms that were discovered and created into mecha.

[]:             Quote Post by Strayn » Tue Mar 15, 2011 9:15 pm Dr. Nick wrote: Which Gundam? There's like 30 different series in the franchise. The three kids seem to be the easiest characters to pinpoint.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Wed Mar 16, 2011 2:18 am After War Gundam X
summary:
[]: Eureka Seven copying Evangelion?             Quote Post by Strayn » Fri Mar 11, 2011 12:45 pm I have noticed that Eureka Seven has quite a few similarities with Evangelion. Has anyone else noticed this, as well?

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Fri Mar 11, 2011 2:29 pm It's been quite common with mecha anime to sort of borrow or spoof Evangelion for a while. Otaku USA
